# EM Frequency Explorer

> An interactive, zoomable explorer for the electromagnetic spectrum — from below
> ELF through radio, microwave, infrared, visible light, ultraviolet, X-ray and
> gamma, all on one continuous logarithmic frequency axis. Shows U.S. (FCC)
> frequency allocations and amateur-radio licence classes. Free, open-source,
> runs in the browser. Built and published by Exagrow (https://exagrow.com/).

Live: https://emfe.exagrow.com/
Source: https://github.com/wajeemba/emfe (Apache-2.0)

## What it does

A single continuous view of the entire electromagnetic spectrum on a logarithmic
frequency (hertz) axis. You can:

- Zoom and pan from mains hum (~60 Hz) up to gamma rays (~10^20 Hz).
- Toggle "content layers" that group allocations by audience:
  consumer/everyday, amateur radio, navigation/aviation, government/satellite,
  and physical science.
- Filter by amateur-radio operator licence class (FCC 47 CFR §97).
- Inspect any band for its allocation, services, and licensing.
- Deep-link any view via the URL query string (zoom, centre, layers, licence,
  theme, selection) — shared links reproduce the exact view.

## Underlying data

U.S. electromagnetic-spectrum allocations and licence classes, derived from the
FCC tables, presented on a continuous logarithmic axis. Allocations are bundled
with the app (committed JSON snapshots); live FCC data is used when available and
falls back to the snapshot otherwise.

## Common questions

- **What is this for?** Understanding what occupies each part of the EM spectrum
  — what we broadcast on, navigate by, and see — in one continuous picture.
- **Whose allocations are shown?** The United States (FCC), including amateur
  licence classes.
- **Is it free?** Yes — free to use and open-source (Apache-2.0).
- **Who made it?** Exagrow (https://exagrow.com/), an AI consulting and systems
  firm in the Minneapolis–St. Paul area.

## Related

- Exagrow (publisher): https://exagrow.com/
